blocks.text: LIN for DC-DC converters uses a master-slave system: only one control unit becomes the master control unit (PGM-FI ECU) and manages all signals on the LIN bus. The slave unit (DC-DC converter) functions by signals from the master node. If the signal sent using LIN cannot be received for a predetermined period of time or if the information sent using LIN remains abnormal for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines that a failure has occurred and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
When one of the following conditions continues for more than 5 seconds

items.text: When the PGM-FI ECU cannot receive signals from the DC-DC converter sent using LIN.
When the PGM-FI ECU receives an error signal from the DC-DC converter sent using LIN.
Disconnection of cord (LIN line) between PGM-FI ECU and DC-DC converter
Ground fault in cord (LIN line) between PGM-FI ECU and DC-DC converter

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
